mnemonics: Exec Family Replaces Current Process Image (EXCRPI)
IPC: Pipes, Signals, Message Queues, Semaphores, Shared Memory (Please Signal My Super Smart Helper)
keyPoints: The exec() family of functions replaces the current process image with a new one.
exec() does not create a new process; the process ID (PID) remains the same.
Inter-Process Communication (IPC) methods allow processes to exchange data and synchronize actions.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that a successful call to exec() never returns; any code after it will not run unless the call fails.
Confusing fork() (which creates a new process) with exec() (which replaces the current process).
Assuming that a process's environment (like open file descriptors) is always preserved across an exec() call; it depends on the specific variant used (e.g., execle() vs. execlp()).
